channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Drones Strike St. Petersburg as Putin Opens Economic Forum
item.description: Ukrainian long-range drones targeted oil storage facilities near Russia's second-largest city on the opening day of Vladimir Putin's flagship St. Petersburg International Economic Forum. The strike came as Zelensky said Ukraine had hit a navy base and oil terminal.

item.title: 🇮🇶 Iran-Backed Iraqi Militias Agree to Hand Over Weapons
item.description: Powerful Iran-backed militias in Iraq announced they will begin handing in weapons to authorities, a significant development in regional de-escalation efforts.

item.title: 💰 US Announces 10%+ Tariffs on Most Trading Partners Over Forced Labor
item.description: The Trump administration announced plans for additional tariffs of 10% or more on most US trading partners following a forced labor investigation. The move affects allies including the UK, EU, Canada, Australia and Taiwan.

item.title: 🇮🇹 Four Migrant Workers Burned Alive in Italy Car Attack
item.description: Four migrant workers were reportedly burned alive in their car in Calabria, Italy, in an attack that highlights the widespread exploitation of foreign farm laborers in southern Europe.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Protests Over Murder Case Turn Violent, 11 Officers Wounded
item.description: Eleven police officers were wounded in Southampton as demonstrators pelted them with rocks and garbage cans during protests over a murder case. British leaders urged calm.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Drones Strike St Petersburg Oil Terminal During Putin Economic Forum
item.description: Ukrainian drones struck oil facilities in St Petersburg, setting an oil terminal ablaze as the city hosts an annual economic forum that Putin is scheduled to address. The attack disrupts Russia's flagship business event.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli Airstrikes Hit Southern Lebanon Despite Partial Ceasefire With Hezbollah
item.description: Israel continued its campaign in southern Lebanon with airstrikes, though it has not struck Beirut after a US-announced deal. The partial truce appears to be holding in the capital but fighting persists in border regions.

item.title: 🇭🇺 Orban Ordered Seizure of Ukrainian Gold Shipment
item.description: Hungarian PM Viktor Orban personally ordered the seizure of a Ukrainian bank convoy in retaliation for the suspension of oil transit through the Druzhba pipeline, according to Hungarian media reports.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Launches New Attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain
item.description: Iran conducted fresh drone and missile attacks targeting Kuwait and Bahrain as US Central Command conducted self-defense strikes on an Iranian military ground control station on Qeshm Island.

item.title: 🇩🇰 Denmark Gets First Government With Majority Women Ministers
item.description: For the first time in Danish history, the new cabinet will consist of more women than men as the PM presents a new government coalition.

item.title: ☢️ US May Consider Placing Nuclear Weapons in Poland and Baltic States
item.description: While discussions are ongoing, an agreement to expand US nuclear hosting was not imminent, according to an unnamed source cited by the Financial Times.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Seven Killed as Drone Strikes Bus in Russian-Controlled Crimea
item.description: A bus traveling between Moscow and Simferopol in Russian-occupied Crimea was hit by a drone attack, killing seven people. The strike highlights the expanding scope of drone warfare in the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iranian Drone Strike Kills One at Kuwait International Airport
item.description: Iran launched drone strikes on Kuwait's main international airport, killing one person and wounding dozens. Iran said the attack was retaliation for earlier US strikes on an Iranian oil tanker and island.

item.title: 🌍 UK-China Relations Thaw as West Needs Beijing
item.description: British Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper hailed 'candour and respect' in new ties with Beijing, highlighting a pragmatic shift in Western diplomacy toward China despite ongoing tensions.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Strikes Russian Warship in Baltic Fleet
item.description: Ukrainian forces successfully struck a Russian warship in the Baltic Fleet, expanding the conflict beyond the Black Sea theater and demonstrating Ukraine's long-range strike capabilities.

item.title: 🇫🇷 France Restricts Israeli Presence at Europe's Biggest Defense Show
item.description: France imposed restrictions on Israeli participation at Eurosatory, Europe's largest defense exhibition, amid ongoing tensions over the Gaza conflict and broader Middle East situation.

item.title: 🇰🇼 Kuwait Airport Hit by Iranian Attack
item.description: Kuwait's international airport was struck by Iranian drones as regional clashes continue. The attack comes as diplomatic efforts between Washington and Tehran show little progress.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel-Lebanon Updates: Netanyahu and Trump Discuss Ceasefire
item.description: Ongoing coverage of the Israel-Lebanon conflict, including diplomatic efforts involving Trump and Netanyahu over potential ceasefire arrangements and regional de-escalation.

item.title: 🇳🇴 Norway Joins French Nuclear Deterrence Program
item.description: Norway announced its participation in France's nuclear deterrence framework, a significant shift in European security architecture as Nordic nations deepen defense cooperation.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Reza Pahlavi Faces Scrutiny Over Shah's Secret Police Legacy
item.description: Far-right praise for the shah's secret police Savak has put Reza Pahlavi, heir to the Iranian throne, in an uncomfortable spotlight as Iran's opposition movement navigates its post-revolution identity.

item.title: 🇨🇺 Cuba's Medical Diplomacy Program Faces Scrutiny in Latin America
item.description: Cuba's decades-long medical diplomacy program, which sends doctors abroad, is facing increased scrutiny and criticism across Latin America over working conditions and political motivations.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Where Is Iran's Highly Enriched Uranium?
item.description: The fate of Iran's stockpile is among the biggest uncertainties surrounding a potential peace deal between the United States and Iran, as nuclear negotiations continue behind closed doors.

item.title: 🇮🇶 Israel's Invasion Devastates Centuries of Lebanese Heritage
item.description: From Phoenician temples to Crusader castles, heritage sites are bearing the brunt of Israel's expanding military offensive in southern Lebanon, raising concerns about cultural destruction.

item.title: 🇮🇳 Fire at New Delhi Hotel Kills at Least 21
item.description: At least 21 people were killed when a fire ripped through a hotel in New Delhi, including 18 foreign nationals who had travelled to India for medical treatment.

item.title: ⚽ FIFA Faces Lawsuit Over Dallas Whale Mural Destruction
item.description: An American artist is suing FIFA over the destruction of a nearly three-decade-old whale mural in Dallas that was painted over for World Cup promotional displays.
